title: grindelia rubricaulis var. bracteosa
intro: Grindelia rubricaulis var. bracteosa, also known as gumweed, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor burns. The health benefits of grindelia rubricaulis var. bracteosa include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and protecting against infections. Herbal preparations of grindelia rubricaulis var. bracteosa are typically made into salves, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or taken orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
